title: [BE] Refactor Login & Registration

summary.blocker_reason: OAuth tests require real credentials (Google JWT token, Telegram bot token)

preparation.notes: HMAC-SHA256 implementation for Telegram WebApp initData
Mock JWT generator (backend requires real Google-signed tokens)

test_results.name: Traditional Email/Password Login

response.description: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.

validation: ⚠️ Registration works, login fails - inconsistent state

test_results.name: Existing Google User Login
test_results.reason: Requires real Google JWT token signed by Google API

api_response.notes: Backend accepted mock token structure but returned null (token validation skipped or silent fail)

test_results.requirement: Need: (1) Real Google OAuth credential via browser login, OR (2) Test mode endpoint that accepts mock tokens

test_results.name: Existing Telegram User Login
test_results.requirement: Need: (1) Bot token, (2) Test Telegram user ID, OR (3) Existing test account credentials

test_results.name: Pre-Migration Google User Backfill
test_results.reason: Requires pre-migration test account + real Google token

test_results.name: Pre-Migration Telegram User Backfill
test_results.reason: Requires pre-migration test account + real Telegram hash

blockers.description: OAuth tests cannot be executed without real credentials
blockers.root_cause: Backend validates OAuth tokens against Google/Telegram APIs. Mock tokens are rejected or silently ignored.
solutions: Option 1: Ihor provides real test credentials (Google test account + Telegram bot token)
Option 2: Backend team adds test mode to accept mock tokens on dev environment
Option 3: Manual UI testing via browser to capture real OAuth tokens

blockers.description: Login endpoint throws NullReferenceException after successful registration
blockers.root_cause: Backend bug - possibly related to OAuth refactor breaking traditional auth flow
blockers.recommendation: Report to backend team immediately - this blocks all regression testing

findings.title: Login fails with NullReferenceException
findings.description: After successful registration, login with same credentials throws 'Object reference not set to an instance of an object.'
steps_to_reproduce: 2. Attempt login with same email/password via /{partnerId}/api/v3/Client/Login
findings.expected: Login should succeed and return session token
findings.actual: GeneralException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object

findings.title: Google OneTapAuth accepts mock tokens silently
findings.description: When sending mock JWT to OneTapAuth endpoint, backend returns Success with null ResponseObject instead of validation error
findings.implication: Either token validation is disabled on dev, or backend silently fails and returns success

deliverables.description: Regression test script for email/password auth

next_steps.description: Create Jira ticket for login NullReferenceException
Request from Ihor: (1) Google test account OAuth token, (2) Telegram bot token + test user ID
Once credentials available, run full OAuth test suite
Verify if login bug exists on QA or is dev-specific

recommendations: Backend team should add test mode flag to accept mock OAuth tokens on dev/stage environments
Consider adding API endpoint to create test users with pre-populated ExternalId for backfill testing
Investigate why OneTapAuth returns Success with null object - should fail fast on invalid tokens
